title: Plastic waste generation by region and polymer
subtitle: Polymer types are as follows: Acrylonitrile Butadiene (AB), Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA), Styrene Acrylonitrile (SAN), Polyurethane (PUR), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), Polystyrene (PS), Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET), High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).
caption:
Plastic waste generation by region and polymer, 2019

Polymer types are as follows: Acrylonitrile Butadiene (AB), Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA), Styrene Acrylonitrile (SAN), Polyurethane (PUR), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), Polystyrene (PS), Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET), High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).

This stacked bar chart displays the regional composition of plastic waste generation across thirteen distinct polymer categories for the year 2019. Each horizontal bar represents a specific polymer type, with the total length extending to 100 percent, segmented by color to show the proportional contribution of ten geographic regions including Europe, China, Asia (excluding China and India), the Middle East & North Africa, India, Sub-Saharan Africa, the Americas (excluding USA), Oceania, the United States, and others. The United States, represented by the teal segment on the far right, consistently accounts for a substantial share of waste generation across nearly all polymer types, often comprising between 15 and 25 percent of the total for categories such as PVC, PUR, and PS. Europe, shown in blue on the left side of the bars, and China, shown in maroon adjacent to it, also represent major contributors, with Europe frequently holding the largest single share in categories like road marking coatings and bioplastics. In contrast, regions such as Oceania and Sub-Saharan Africa occupy very narrow bands within the stacks, indicating minimal relative generation volumes compared to the larger economies. The distribution of waste generation varies slightly by polymer; for instance, the Americas (excluding USA) and India show visible increases in their shares for polymers like HDPE and PET compared to their shares in elastomers or marine coatings. The visual arrangement highlights that a small number of regions dominate the global plastic waste profile across the entire spectrum of material types, while the remaining regions contribute smaller, consistent fractions regardless of the specific polymer involved.
